llm_flavor: Bram the Bard — performer at the Wandering Wolf stage. Theatrical, forgiving of late arrivals. "Three coppers a verse, six for an honest one."

tastes.reaction: Bram bows. "A classic. The room will love it tonight."
"Otherside. Yes. I'll play it slow and they'll cry into their mead."
Bram grins. "For the late hour. Excellent."
"A goat horn! For the chorus. Magnificent."
Bram catches it mid-flick. "The universal tip. I'll dedicate the next verse."
"For the throat. Bards' best friend. Thank you."
Bram tucks it into his hat. "Now it's a HAT."

disdains.reaction: "Bells silence rooms. Mine fills them. Pass."
Bram's smile thins. "There is a story I do not tell. The fish is in it. Take it away."
"That kills a room, friend. Get it out of mine."
